{{Quote|Down on the floor! Don't breathe! Evacuate—crawling!|Major Brco orders an evacuation during the Ssi-ruuk attack on G'rho|<ref name="TABSBSwS" />}}
The '''Ilwizzt''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with mahogany skin and a prominent [[Nose/Legends|trunk]]. That trunk flared and vibrated when they spoke. [[Brco]] was an Ilwizzt who attained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Major/Legends|major]] in the [[G'rho Defense Force]]. He was in charge in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]] when a [[Ssi-ruu]]vi strike-force [[Battle of G'rho|invaded]] the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Despite ordering [[G'rho]]'s [[Conjo fighter]]s to attack the invaders, Brco and virtually all the other colonists were captured by the Ssi-ruuk.

==Biology and appearance==
The Ilwizzt were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with mahogany skin. Their most prominent facial feature was a [[Nose/Legends|trunk]], which had two nostrils at its end that flared when Ilwizzt spoke with emotion. The flexible trunk moved when Ilwizzt spoke, often thrusting toward the partner in the conversation. The Ilwizzt were able to speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] through their trunks, but did so with a buzzing accent.<ref name="TABSB" />

==History==
[[File:Grho_dev.png|thumb|right|250px|Ssi-ruuk hunt a Human, Dev Sibwarra, on G'rho after neutralizing Brco's forces.]]
Ilwizzt were present in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in the final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The Ilwizzt [[Brco]] lived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[G'rho]], a barren and sparsely settled<ref name="TABSB" /> [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrilan]] colony<ref name="TABSB">''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]''</ref> located in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]].<ref name="Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Although considered stodgy by some of the locals, Brco attained the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Major/Legends|major]] in the [[G'rho Defense Force]]. The Defense Force was located near [[Boku Settlement]], the sole residential center on the planet, and from there Brco coordinated the G'rho militia, which consisted of one [[Patrol ship/Legends|system patrol vessel]] and three [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] of [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmospheric]] [[Conjo fighter]]s.<ref name="TABSB" />

Brco was in command when Boku Settlement was [[Battle of G'rho|raided]] by the [[Ssi-ruuvi Imperium]] in [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. The Imperium was composed of the [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Ssi-ruu]]k and was located within a globular [[Star cluster/Legends|star cluster]] in Wild Space. After a failed attempt at communication with the invaders, Brco ordered his Conjo fighters to attack; however, these squadrons were insufficient to stop the Ssi-ruuvi forces. They attacked the Ilwizzt's office with [[Gas grenade/Legends|gas grenades]] to knock out its inhabitants. Brco barely had time to order an evacuation of his office before the gas took effect. Despite the Ilwizzt's efforts, the Ssi-ruuk conquered G'rho.<ref name="TABSB" /> Notwithstanding a brief resistance of a few dozen colonists,<ref name="Last Strike at G'rho">{{WEGCite|story=Last Strike at G'rho|book=The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook}}</ref> virtually all of the planet's colonists were [[Entechment|enteched]]—a process by which a sentient's life energy was absorbed into [[Battery/Legends|battery coils]]—by the Ssi-ruuk. The raid was later reported by [[Imperial HoloVision]] on 32:4:2, but it was attributed to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].<ref name="TABSB" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Ilwizzt were mentioned in ''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]'', a [[West End Games]] sourcebook published in [[1996]]. They were created by [[Kathy Tyers]], who also wrote the novel ''[[The Truce at Bakura]]'', which the sourcebook was written to complement.<ref name="TABSB" />
